routineText:
09:00 — Wake up & go to Work
09:00
      — (4h) Work & Water 1L
13:00
13:00 — Black coffee + 1 tsp of sugar

-----------------------
| 14:00 — 🌅 Breakfast
| — whole wheat bread 35g
| — 6 whites & 3 yolks of beldi eggs fried with 5g of olive oil
| — 5g of raw olive oil
| — mint tea + 1 tsp of sugar
| — cumin & salt
| — oat 55g + warm water
| — 1 scoop of protein powder with water
| — 1/2 small apple
| — 1/4 small peach
| — 1/2 small kiwi
| — 2 garlic cloves
| — 15g pumpkin seeds
-----------------------

14:00 — return to Work
14:00
      — (4h) Work & Water 1L
18:00
18:00 — Out from work

-----------------------
| 18:00 — ☀️ Lunch
| — chicken breast 233g raw, skinless, cooked with 1 small onion
| — brown rice 10g raw
| — 300g legume sauté with 17g of olive oil (carrot, courgette, onion)
| — cumin & salt
-----------------------

18:00 — Black Coffee
18:00
      — (4h) Rest or Billard & Water 1L
21:00
21:00 — Back Home

-----------------------
| 21:00 — 🌙 Dinner
| — 1 scoop of protein powder
| — 1 perly nature
| — 2g Almonds
| — 2g Walnuts
| — 2g Cashews
| — 2g Pistachios
| — 1 kiwi
| — cumin & salt
-----------------------

01:00 — Sleep

adjustments.reason: Rest-day carb target is ~130g. Bread (breakfast) + oat 55g + rice 70g would land carbs at 124% (161g), on the edge of the warn zone. Trimming rice by 25g — the lowest-fiber-density carb source in the meal — brings carbs to 110% (right at the good-range ceiling) and calories to almost exactly 1,800, without touching protein or fat.
Protocol's anti-gas 'do' rule calls for cumin in every meal — gas/bloating is the #1 flagged issue. Mehdi confirmed cumin was added at lunch; dinner still has none.

The post-breakfast apple, peach, and kiwi pushed carbs to 123% (warn). A further rice trim (45g→25g) removes ~14.5g carbs, bringing carbs down to 112% — not quite back to 'good' but much closer — while calories land almost exactly on the 1,800 target.

adjustments.to: Chicken breast 233g raw, skinless + brown rice 10g; legume sauté oil increased 5g→17g
adjustments.reason: Requested: rebuild lunch + dinner (breakfast untouched) so the full day lands at exactly 100% of every target. With breakfast fixed, the remaining protein/carb/fat budget for lunch+dinner was too tight for kefta's fat:protein ratio — hitting 90.7g protein without blowing the 32.8g fat budget only works with a much leaner protein. Chicken breast (2.6g fat/100g vs. kefta's 8g/100g) closes the exact gap; rice drops to 10g since the carb budget left after breakfast's bread/oat/fruit is only ~53g. Trade-off: B12 drops from 214% to 12.5% and iron/copper both fall out of the good range — kefta was carrying those, chicken doesn't replace them.

verdict.text_html: All four numbers essentially exact — 1,803 kcal (100%), protein 100%, carbs 100%, fat 102% — but swapping lunch's kefta for chicken breast to make the fat budget work cost real ground on B12, iron, and copper.

coverage.name: Calories
coverage.note: Rebuilding lunch/dinner around the exact remaining budget lands this almost dead-on

coverage.name: Protein
coverage.note: Chicken breast 233g solves for exactly the protein still needed after breakfast

coverage.name: Carbs
coverage.note: Rice cut to 10g — the carb budget left after bread, oat, and breakfast fruit was very tight

coverage.name: Fat
coverage.note: Only hits exactly because kefta (8g fat/100g) was replaced with lean chicken breast (2.6g fat/100g)

coverage.name: Fiber
coverage.note: Legume sauté + oat + fruit carry this — rice cut to 10g cost a little fiber too

coverage.name: Gas / bloat risk
coverage.note: Cumin was only at breakfast, now added to lunch too — dinner still has none; onion is cooked (not raw) in both dishes, which is fine per protocol

coverage.name: Protocol adherence
coverage.note: Bread once ✓, dinner well before 22:00 ✓ — cumin now covered at breakfast + lunch, dinner still missing it

coverage.name: Vitamin C
coverage.note: Apple + peach + kiwi at breakfast pushed this well past the dinner kiwi + vegetables alone

coverage.name: Calcium
coverage.note: No milk (protocol dont) and only one perly today — weakest spot of the day

good: All three macros land at exactly 100% (protein, carbs) or 102% (fat) — the tightest fit of any day logged so far
Vitamin C still strong at 160% — apple, peach, and kiwi at breakfast on top of the dinner kiwi
Cumin now added to both lunch and dinner, closing the gap flagged on every prior day
bad: Vitamin B12 collapses from 214% to ~12.5% — chicken breast doesn't carry anywhere near the B12 that kefta did; the exact macro fit cost a nutrient that was previously one of the day's strongest
Iron drops from 98% (good) to ~57% (warn), and Copper drops from 41% to ~23% (bad) — same root cause, kefta was quietly doing a lot of micronutrient work
Rice cut to just 10g to make the carbs fit exactly — a token portion, not a real side
Calcium (16%), Iodine (13%), Chromium (14%) — structural gaps on a day with no dairy, fatty fish, or avocado
loggedItems: 6 egg whites + 3 egg yolks (beldi), fried with 5g olive oil
Chicken breast 233g raw, skinless, cooked with 1 small onion (~50g)
Brown rice 10g raw (rebuilt from 70g to hit the exact carb budget)
300g légumes sautés (carrot 100g, courgette 100g, onion 100g) with 17g olive oil

fullTable.note: Chicken breast 233g + 2 whey scoops + eggs + pumpkin seeds — exactly on target
3 × 1L blocks from the schedule
Oat, legumes, and the breakfast apple/peach/kiwi contribute, but rice down to 10g and no potato/banana keep this short
Beldi eggs' richer yolks + walnuts 2g — better than plain eggs but still low, no fatty fish
3 egg yolks only — structural gap without fatty fish
Chicken breast carries far less iron than kefta did — dropped from 98% (good) after the protein swap
Chicken breast carries only a fraction of kefta's B12 — this was 214% (very strong) before the swap, now the day's biggest single loss
Pumpkin seeds still the biggest single source, but chicken + 10g rice carry less magnesium than kefta + 25g rice did
No milk (protocol dont) — only one perly nature today, weakest spot of the day
Chicken breast carries much less zinc than kefta — dropped from 162% (good) after the protein swap
Chicken + vegetables + kiwi + perly + the breakfast fruit, but no potato/banana today
Oat, rice, vegetables, kiwi, yolks, and the breakfast fruit each contribute a little
Apple + peach + kiwi at breakfast on top of the dinner kiwi + vegetables push this well past target
Carrot + beldi egg yolks (richer in Vit A than standard eggs)
Structural gap — no dairy, seafood, or iodized salt today
3 olive oil portions + almonds, but no avocado today — lower than usual
Kiwi (breakfast + dinner) + carrot + courgette — no avocado today to help
Chicken breast is actually a strong B6 source — pushes this even higher than kefta did
Chicken breast is an even stronger selenium source than kefta — comfortably the best-covered nutrient today
Oat and bread carry most of this now — rice down to 10g and chicken contributes less than kefta did
Eggs (whites + yolks) carry most of this — chicken contributes a bit less than kefta did
Chicken breast is a much stronger niacin source than kefta — one of the clear wins of the swap
Chicken carries far less copper than kefta — dropped out of the warn range after the protein swap
3 egg yolks carry most of this — chicken adds less than kefta did, dropping from 103% to 94%
Fromage frais mild — consider swapping for raib for better gut value
Bread + chicken — comfortably within normal range, slightly lower than kefta gave
Carrot, courgette, kiwi, apple, peach, olive oil, nuts — the breakfast fruit broadened this from earlier
Eggs + chicken + perly comfortably clear the target — chicken's phosphorus is on par with kefta's
Oat + nuts carry most of this — rice down to 10g contributes only a little now
Oat and rice are the only grain sources — lower than days with potato/banana
Egg yolks + chicken breast cover this comfortably — chicken is actually a slightly better B5 source than kefta
3 egg yolks provide the bulk of this
